task: The horse galloped across the field at sunset. It had been trained for show jumping competitions.
Wendy met Justine at the library. She had reserved a study room for the entire afternoon.
The boat drifted away from the dock overnight. It had not been tied properly the previous evening.
Bernard helped Reginald carry the luggage. He had injured his back the previous week.
The painter mixed the pigments in the studio. He achieved a shade he had been seeking for weeks.
Phoebe consulted Eleanor about the manuscript. She offered detailed and constructive feedback on the entire draft.
The candle melted onto the wooden table. It had burned for many hours unattended in the dim room.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the wooden table".

task: Sebastian thanked Lawrence for the recommendation. He had received a job offer the very next week.
The radio played the old song from the speaker. It reminded her of summers in the countryside.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the old song".
